content: Black beans are high in antioxidants, which help fight free radicals and improve nutrient absorption.
Black beans are rich in fiber, which reduces levels of C-reactive protein (CRP) and helps reduce inflammation.
Black beans have a starchy texture that provides satiety and helps level blood sugar due to their high fiber content.
To mitigate the impact of the lectin inhibitor in black beans, consume foods that digest quickly before black beans.
Eating protein-rich foods first followed by slower-digesting black beans can help avoid the inhibition caused by lectins in black beans.
